title: Egg mayo and cress sandwich
subtitle: Soft boiled egg mayonnaise with mustard cress on white bread
excerpt: Egg mayonnaise and cress is the British office sandwich at its best. Hard-boiled eggs mixed with mayonnaise and a little mustard, piled onto white bread with a scatter of garden cress. It is mild, creamy, and a very good sandwich.
sourceNotes: Standard British lunchtime sandwich found in every supermarket, cafe, and sandwich bar. Egg mayonnaise sandwiches have been a fixture of British sandwich culture since the mid-20th century and remain one of the most popular sandwich fillings in the country.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The egg mayonnaise filling can be made a day ahead and kept chilled. Add the cress just before assembling.

content.text: Egg mayonnaise sandwiches are best made with eggs that are fully set but not overcooked: the yolk should crumble and mix into the mayonnaise rather than turn grey. Mash rather than chop the eggs for the best texture. A little English mustard in the mayonnaise sharpens the filling. The cress goes in last to keep it fresh.
Place the eggs in a small saucepan, cover with cold water, and bring to the boil. Reduce to a steady simmer and cook for 9 minutes. Drain and run under cold water for 2 minutes to stop cooking. Peel.
Mash the eggs with a fork in a bowl. Add the mayonnaise and mustard and mix well. Season with black pepper and a little salt if needed. The mixture should be soft and spreadable.
Butter the bread. Spread the egg mixture over two slices. Scatter cress over the filling. Top with the remaining slices, press lightly, and cut in half to serve.

items.symptom: The yolk has a grey ring around it
items.fix: 9 minutes is the maximum for hard-boiled eggs. Cool immediately in cold water. The grey ring does not affect taste but is a sign of overcooking.

items.cause: Too much mayonnaise, or eggs were still warm when mixed.
items.fix: Cool the eggs fully before mixing. Use 2 tablespoons of mayonnaise for 4 eggs, adding more only if needed.

items.symptom: The cress turns wilted by the time it is eaten
items.cause: Cress added too early or the sandwich was left uncovered.
items.fix: Keep the assembled sandwich covered or wrapped. Add the cress at the last moment if making ahead.

content.text: Egg mayonnaise with cress has been a popular British sandwich filling since the 1970s, when supermarket counters and pre-packed sandwiches made it widely available. The cress connects it to the older British habit of growing cress on a windowsill in a seed tray. It is mild and filling. It regularly tops surveys of British sandwich preferences.
